sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 55.5g of fat. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: portable, practical snack food because nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use. fat and texture help satiety because they can hold up better than many snack foods. still a recognisable whole-food option because that keeps them cleaner than most processed snacks.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: spread form makes overdoing it easier because still good, but less self-limiting than whole nuts. protein is useful but not elite because nuts help, but they are not protein stars. cost or allergy issues can limit use because practical downsides still count.

closing.summary: Almond Butter (Unsweetened) does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
